title: PROJECT_BLOCK_TYPES const at project-sdk.ts:117 hardcodes 12 default block kinds — should be config-derived for vocabulary-neutrality (DEC-0025)
description: packages/pi-context/src/project-sdk.ts lines 117-130 declares: export const PROJECT_BLOCK_TYPES = ['project', 'domain', 'requirements', 'architecture', 'tasks', 'decisions', 'issues', 'rationale', 'verification', 'handoff', 'conformance-reference', 'audit'] as const. This list is consumed downstream as the canonical default block-kind set shipped with /project init. Per DEC-0025 (vocabulary-neutral substrate canon), built-in vocabulary literals in source are gaps to close — the framework's source code must operate on canonical_id-keyed registries derived purely from loaded config. A user with a wholly different substrate conception (e.g. {initiatives, OKRs, experiments, decisions, tasks, runs, observations}) cannot use pi-context as-is without either (a) accepting these 12 defaults as installed even when irrelevant or (b) modifying source to remove or replace them. The const is also a regression risk: any future addition to the const adds an implicit framework commitment.

evidence.reference: Lines 117-130: PROJECT_BLOCK_TYPES const with 12 hardcoded canonical_ids
Line 1707: re-exports PROJECT_BLOCK_TYPES (consumed by /project init + tooling)
DEC-0025 declares vocabulary-neutrality principle; this const violates it
Default block instances shipped under /registry — those are the proper home for shipped defaults; the const duplicates their listing

impact: Vocabulary-neutrality claim of DEC-0025 partially undelivered. User cannot adopt pi-context with wholly-different substrate conception purely through config + schemas + macros — the const remains as a framework default-vocabulary commitment in source. Tools relying on PROJECT_BLOCK_TYPES (e.g. /project init scaffolding) cannot scaffold a fully-custom substrate without code change. Cross-package consumers (pi-workflows, pi-behavior-monitors, pi-jit-agents) that reference PROJECT_BLOCK_TYPES inherit the same coupling.
proposed_resolution: Replace PROJECT_BLOCK_TYPES const with config-derived equivalent: (a) /project init (or future /context init) reads packages/pi-context/registry/blocks/ + registry/schemas/ at install time + lets user select which to install via prompt or flag; (b) downstream tooling that iterates PROJECT_BLOCK_TYPES instead reads from loaded config.installed_blocks[]; (c) the const, if retained, becomes a documentation-only export naming the canonical-default kinds shipped under registry — not a source-of-truth for what is installed. Cascade: audit all PROJECT_BLOCK_TYPES consumers (grep across packages/* + scripts/*); each consumer migrates to config.installed_blocks[] read OR registry directory enumeration; tests updated to exercise non-default substrate vocabularies (FGAP-058). Cross-decision with FGAP-052 (config-declared-but-unconsumed runtime gaps) — same root principle.
